page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-96 is a compute-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor. It features 96 vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The instance is configured with 192 GB of DDR5 memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware. The processor features an extensive instruction set, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations.

Benchmark evaluations place this instance in the top tier for multi-threaded operations and cryptographic tasks. It achieves top-decile performance in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, Geekbench single-core and multi-core evaluations, and PassMark database operations. Cryptographic performance is highly competitive, as demonstrated by top-tier PassMark CPU encryption scores. Memory bandwidth is strong across both small and large block reads, though single-core CPU performance and memory latency metrics fall into the average tier.

Given its high compute density and lack of bundled local storage, the c4-highcpu-96 is highly cost-efficient for compute-bound workloads that do not require massive memory-to-CPU ratios. It is well-suited for high-throughput static web serving, multi-threaded data compression, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference. The presence of AMX instructions makes it a viable option for running medium-sized language model inference tasks efficiently without dedicated GPU hardware.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-96 is a compute-optimized server instance designed for high-throughput, CPU-intensive workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores, it provides 192 GB of RAM, translating to 2.0 GB of memory per core. The architecture features Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512, which drive top-tier performance in multi-threaded decompression, database operations, and cryptographic encryption. While it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, its strong memory bandwidth and high compute density make it highly efficient for parallel processing. It is optimized for workloads such as software compilation, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-96 is a compute-optimized server featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB of RAM, and advanced AMX instructions, delivering top-tier performance for database and cryptographic workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-96 is a compute-optimized server with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 192 GB RAM, built for high-throughput parallel workloads.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized server featuring ninety-six vCPUs and advanced matrix extensions for accelerated parallel processing and cryptographic workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 192 GB RAM powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor
Top-tier performance in multi-threaded decompression and database operations
Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated CPU-based machine learning inference
Strong memory bandwidth and high-throughput static web serving capabilities
Optimized for compute-bound workloads, software compilation, and cryptography
